principles: Keep the existing asynchronous factory and legacy solve(problem, options) contract.
Expose stable non-deprecated C API capability through raw status-returning methods and a persistent throwing Model.
Never expose native pointers, live WebAssembly views, filesystem paths, or arbitrary calls.
Copy all public array results into detached JavaScript-owned typed arrays.
Use exact snake_case HiGHS option names and reject file, thread, parallel, and concurrency options in the new API.

replace.reason: The C function returns bytes, so the proposed name makes 32 versus 64 ambiguous.
A small explicit lifecycle maps cleanly to native ownership.

replace.proposal: Model.fromFile, writeModel, writeSolution, writeOptions and other path methods
replace.backing: corresponding Highs_read* / Highs_write* calls through hidden temporary MEMFS files
replace.reason: Public filesystem paths are environment-dependent and unsafe in browser workers.
There is no Highs_getCoeff C function; sparse extraction is faster when callers need more than one coefficient.

with: const snapshot = model.getModel(); const copy = highs.createModel(snapshot)
replace.reason: The C API has no clone and an explicit copy makes its O(model size) cost and memory peak visible.
Raw parity must preserve exact status values; high-level errors can add readable messages.
An arbitrary escape hatch cannot validate pointer ownership, lengths, integer overflow, or callback safety.

omit.alternative: Every owning object has dispose(); implementations may additionally define Symbol.dispose when available.
omit.reason: Requiring esnext.disposable needlessly raises the consumer TypeScript lib target.

omit.proposal: algebraic Variable, Constraint, LinearExpression and QuadraticExpression handles
omit.alternative: Use sparse ModelData plus add/change/pass methods.
omit.reason: These are C++-style objects with no stable C handles; implementing them would require a second JS model graph or native shim with substantial memory and invalidation cost.

omit.proposal: stable variable/constraint ids and live collections
omit.alternative: Use numeric indices, names, tagged selections, and detached snapshots.
omit.reason: The C API is index-based and deletion renumbers entries. Stable ids would add bookkeeping and cannot guarantee native parity.

omit.alternative: Return detached Float64Array and Int32Array values.
omit.reason: Emscripten memory growth and solver operations invalidate live views; safe zero-copy semantics are impossible.

omit.alternative: Accept uncompressed LP/MPS string or Uint8Array data; callers decompress before invoking the API.
None; dispose models and call releaseMemory().
omit.reason: The JavaScript build is single-threaded and upstream documents undefined behavior if the scheduler is in use.

omit.proposal: thread, parallel, and concurrency options on the new API
omit.alternative: The legacy HighsOptions members remain accepted for backward compatibility; the new exact option API rejects them.
omit.reason: The WebAssembly build is intentionally single-threaded.
Synchronous single-threaded WebAssembly cannot observe an external JavaScript interrupt while Highs_run is executing.

omit.proposal: PDLP callback and MIP lazy-constraint callback
omit.alternative: Expose only callback types that upstream invokes in this build.
omit.reason: The proposed PDLP payload is not represented by a usable stable C callback contract here, and the lazy callback type is inert upstream.

omit.proposal: presolveStatus, model name getter, standardForm, kappa, illConditioning, suggestedScaling, savedMipSolutions, presolve logs/maps, assessPrimalSolution, matrixImage
omit.alternative: Use the stable capabilities listed in api/highs-c-api.json.
omit.reason: No matching stable non-deprecated C function exists in the bundled HiGHS header.

omit.proposal: readBasis, readSolution, writeBasis, writeInfo, writeRunData, writeIisModel and related writer methods
omit.alternative: Use typed set/get basis and solution methods, scalar info access, and getIis/getIisLp.
omit.reason: No matching stable non-deprecated C function exists in the bundled HiGHS header.
Only pass/add/clear operations exist in the stable C API.

omit.proposal: arbitrary native pointers including RawModelApi.pointer and callback data pointers
omit.alternative: Use owned RawModel objects and copied callback payloads.
omit.reason: Pointer escape prevents lifecycle and memory-safety guarantees.
Deprecated APIs are explicitly outside the parity target.

keepWithShapeChanges.proposal: LP, MIP and QP bulk input
solution, basis, rays, ranging, IIS, crossover and feasibility relaxation

keepWithShapeChanges.backing: Highs_setCallback / Highs_startCallback / Highs_stopCallback plus a native trampoline
